title: Protein expression on the surface of Escherichia coli
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"1gvgl","text":"Display of proteins on the bacterial cell surface has always been an attractive technique for the production of functional cell-anchored proteins, thereby reducing the cost, time and effort related to enzyme purification. Thus, surface display can enable fast and easy screening of protein libraries, e.g. activity variants, or screening of different enzyme substrates, while maintaining a connection between the phenotype and the genotype. Additionally, cells displaying functional enzymes can potentially be used as whole-cell catalysts.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9s53e","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"rfne","text":"This protocol describes how to express proteins on the surface of E. coli BL21 (DE3) using a L-rhamnose inducible expression system. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":66,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bsa2f","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"71vti","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"acqgm","text":"Display of proteins on the bacterial cell surface is very attractive for fast and easy screening of protein variants, because it maintains a connection between the phenotype and the genotype. For some enzymes to be active, it is crucial whether they are cell-anchored via the C- or N-terminus. The choice of display construct is therefore of great importance. In our lab, we use two different constructs:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"e8lho","text":"pBAD42-LppSP-OmpA-TEV-ccdB and pBAD42-ccdB-TEV-C-IgAP","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":10,"length":2},{"style":"italic","offset":22,"length":4},{"style":"italic","offset":38,"length":4}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8d68r","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c23c0","text":"The constructs are adapted from Wendel et al. (2016) A nanobody:GFP bacterial platform that enables functional enzyme display and easy quantification of display capacity. Microb Cell Fact 15:71, DOI 10.1186/s12934-016-0474-y","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"66ir","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"4ju8","text":"Prepare a fresh transformation of your expression vector in E. coli BL21 (DE3) cells.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":60,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Pick a fresh colony of your BL21 (DE3) strain with your expression vector, and inoculate it in LB supplemented with relevant antibiotics. Grow the culture at 37 °C at 250 RPM shaking Overnight . The volume of the overnight culture depends on the volume of the expression culture and should be at least 1/100 of the expression culture
Dilute the overnight culture 1:100 in fresh LB supplemented with relevant antibiotics
Grow the culture at 37 °C with 250 RPM shaking until an OD 600 = 0.5 - 0.6
Induce expression by adding L-rhamnose to a final concentration of 5 millimolar (mM)
Let the culture grow at 30 °C with 250 RPM shaking for up to 20:00:00
Spin the culture down at 4000 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes and discard the supernatant
Resuspend pellet in the desired assay buffer (e.g. 10 millimolar (mM) - 50 millimolar (mM) Tris-HCl)
Run activity assay in liquid or on agar plates.
Spin the culture down at 4000 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes and discard the supernatant
Resuspend pellet in 10 millimolar (mM) Tris-HCl or 100 µL /ODU TEV cleavage buffer ( 10 millimolar (mM) Tris-HCl pH 7.5 , 150 millimolar (mM) NaCl 2 , 0.5 millimolar (mM) EDTA)
Add purified and activated TEV protease
Incubate at Room temperature Overnight (~ 16:00:00 ) It is also possible to shorten the incubation time, so that the protein harvest can still be performed on the same day.
Spin the cells at 5000 x g, 4 °C, 10 minutes and collect the supernatant
Keep the extractions On ice when working with it and at 4 °C for storage
creator: Maja Rennig
